channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Alibaba Releases Open-Source Video Generation AI Models
item.description: Chinese cloud provider Alibaba releases four versions of its video-generation AI model, Wan2.1, as open source for users to download and run on capable PCs.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX prepares for 8th Starship launch, pending FAA approval
item.description: SpaceX is slated to launch its Starship for the eighth time from Boca Chica, Texas, Friday, though plans could be thwarted if the FAA fails to give the green light.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This external Geforce RTX 4090M GPU is the most powerful you can buy right now and creatives will absolutely love it
item.description: The compact yet powerful Morefine G1 delivers RTX 4090M performance for demanding tasks like 8K editing and AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Former Navy comptroller shares how financial management can successfully impact decision-making
item.description: Modern financial systems offer the ideal way to tie budget to strategy and improve decision-making, explains Russ Rumbaugh, who left the Navy in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Venus Williams backs the walking app WeWard
item.description: WeWard, an app that offers real-world rewards for walking, announced Wednesday it’s signed tennis champ Venus Williams as an investor and ambassador for the app.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: At 10, USB-C still hasn’t lived up to its full potential
item.description: Slightly under 10 years ago, when I reviewed a new Apple MacBook, I devoted a surprising percentage of my wordage to its port. Yes, port—it had only one.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Reform doesn’t come easily to the agency that guards the nuclear weapons stockpile
item.description: The National Nuclear Security Administration is among the agencies trying to reform themselves as it requires certain best practices to be in place. The post Reform doesn’t come easily to the agency that guards the nuclear weapons stockpile first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: A must-try Android email upgrade
item.description: Ahem: I have two separate confessions to make, both equally embarrassing. Confession #1 is that I love email. I’m totally not kidding, either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Finally, Samsung's first PCIe 5.0 SSD that you can actually buy is here; after a nearly two-year wait, meet the 9100 Pro
item.description: It took almost two years, but Samsung’s first PCIe 5.0 consumer SSD, the 9100 Pro, has finally arrived.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Get started with async in Python
item.description: Asynchronous programming, or async, is a feature of many modern languages that allows a program to juggle multiple operations without waiting or getting hung up on any one of them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Riyadh Air and IBM forge AI-Driven partnership to revolutionize aviation
item.description: In a groundbreaking move, Riyadh Air has announced a strategic partnership with IBM to integrate AI across its operations, aiming to establish itself as the world’s first digital-native airline.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE is draining the life from the once-vaunted U.S. Digital Service
item.description: The United States Digital Service (USDS), the storied group of Silicon Valley types brought together by Obama to bring government services into the 21st century, will likely never be the same after the arrival of Elon Musk’s so-called Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Senate Democrats press Trump's FTC commissioner nominee over agency independence
item.description: Multiple Senate Democrats on the Commerce, Science and Transportation on Tuesday pressed President Trump's Federal Trade Commissioner nominee, Mark Meador, over the independence of the agency amid concerns the White House could seek to influence the agency's enforcement efforts.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: I’m a tech expert. These are the 5 time-saving tech tips I tell everyone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson presents five secret shortcuts: Adjust sound/vibration, share YouTube moments, fix tabs and tweak brightness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Nvidia’s earnings are important to the entire U.S. stock market
item.description: Nvidia stock’s movement carries more weight on the S&P 500 and other indexes than every company except Apple.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: 3 strategies for carbon-free data centers
item.description: The construction of massive data center campuses is booming, with hyperscalers, colocation providers and large enterprises developing new capacity to support the exploding requirements of AI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Intel Expands Xeon 6 Lineup with New P-Core Processors
item.description: Intel launches new Xeon 6 processors, featuring Performance-cores (P-cores) for high-performance computing and edge devices.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon concedes that Chime SDK makes far more sense than the Chime application itself
item.description: Amazon’s announcement on Wednesday that it is abandoning its Chime collaboration app, while stressing that it will double down on the far more successful Chime software development kit (SDK), was an example of Amazon being Amazon. It knows what it does well, and where to focus.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GenAI is my copilot? Developers weigh pains and gains
item.description: Developers are quicker than most to embrace generative AI tools as part of their workflow. That can mean enhanced productivity and easing cognitive burdens, but it also means learning new technologies while they’re still being built.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Versa Networks launches sovereign SASE, challenging cloud-only security model
item.description: SASE since its inception has typically been deployed in a software-as-a-service (SaaS) model, delivering network security services from the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Network World
